rationale: The citing paper gives Anderson and Simester (2013) a specific functional role in the literature review: it is cited as field-experimental evidence that competitor advertising can increase focal-firm purchases when switching costs are high. This is not a generic name-check; the cited result is summarized and used to define a recent spillover-research stream that the 2016 paper positions itself within. The citing paper then states its own contribution as extending that stream to online ads and advertising intensity effects.
relation_description: The citing paper builds on Anderson and Simester (2013) as prior causal evidence on competitive advertising spillovers and switching-cost conditions. It extends that line by examining online search ads, identifying which rivals gain spillovers, and analyzing how ad frequency changes spillovers and advertiser returns.
